notes: Old skill had `-- <cmd>` pattern in the 'Any other command' row -- enough for the model to correctly infer separator behavior even without explicit flag-separation guidance
New skill achieves 100% vs 50% for old skill -- a 50-point improvement driven by the two new feature evals
Eval 4 (attention indicator) is the most discriminating: old skill scores 0/4 because it has zero awareness of the red-pulsing bell-notification feature
Eval 5 (bidirectional multi-session) is partially discriminating: old skill passes 2/4 -- bidirectional framing was already present but attention indicator and correct `--` syntax were missing
Eval 6 (separator syntax) is non-discriminating: both skill versions score 4/4 -- the old skill's `-- <cmd>` pattern was sufficient for the model to infer correct flag separation
New skill runs are slightly slower (+3.8s avg) -- richer content leads to more thorough responses
The attention indicator assertion (red-pulse, bell character) is the highest-value discriminator -- completely absent from old skill

Eval 6 shows knowledge generalization: the model correctly inferred `--` separator from a partial pattern in the old skill -- the explicit separator guidance may be redundant for this scenario but still valuable for discoverability
